info.name: Google Cloud Platform Google Cloud Resource Manager API
info.description:
Programmatically manage Google Cloud projects, folders, and organizations. The Cloud Resource Manager API enables you to create, read, update, and delete projects, folders, and organizations in the Google Cloud resource hierarchy. It provides the foundation for organizing and governing Google Cloud resources through a hierarchical structure of organizations, folders, and projects.

Contact Support:
 Name: Google Cloud Platform

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Get a Project

variable.description: (Required) The name of the project to retrieve, such as projects/123 or projects/my-project-id. The project ID or number is required.

request.description: Retrieves the project identified by the specified name, such as projects/123 or projects/my-project-id. The caller must have the resourcemanager.projects.get permission on the project.

response.name: Successful response containing the project.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: The caller does not have required permissions.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: The specified project was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Update a Project

query.description: A comma-separated list of fields to update. Only display_name and labels fields can be updated. An empty update mask is not permitted.

variable.description: (Required) The name of the project to update.

request.description: Updates the display name and labels of the project identified by the specified name. Changes to the project are not reflected immediately and may take several minutes to propagate. The caller must have the resourcemanager.projects.update permission on the project.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation that can be polled for completion.

response.name: Invalid update parameters.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Delete a Project

variable.description: (Required) The name of the project to delete.

request.description: Marks the project identified by the specified name to be deleted. This method sets the project lifecycle state to DELETE_REQUESTED. The project is not immediately deleted but enters a 30-day grace period during which it can be restored using undelete. After 30 days, the project and all its resources are permanently deleted.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation that can be polled for completion.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: The caller does not have required permissions.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: The specified project was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Undelete a Project

request.description: Restores the project identified by the specified name. A project can only be restored within the 30-day grace period after deletion. The caller must have the resourcemanager.projects.undelete permission on the project.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation that can be polled for completion.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: The specified project was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: The project is not in DELETE_REQUESTED state or the 30-day grace period has expired.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

Maximum number of projects to return in the response. The server can return fewer results. If unspecified, the server picks an appropriate default value.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Create a Project

request.description: Creates a new project resource under the specified parent. The parent must be an organization or folder. The caller must have the resourcemanager.projects.create permission on the parent. This method returns a long-running Operation which can be used to track creation.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation that can be polled for completion.

Maximum number of projects to return.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Delete a Folder

variable.description: (Required) The resource name of the folder to delete.

request.description: Requests deletion of a folder. The folder is moved into the DELETE_REQUESTED state and enters a 30-day grace period. During this period, the folder can be restored. After the grace period, the folder is permanently deleted. The folder must be empty before it can be deleted. The caller must have the resourcemanager.folders.delete permission on the folder.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Authentication credentials are missing or invalid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: The specified folder was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Undelete a Folder

request.description: Cancels the deletion request for a folder. This method can only be called within the 30-day grace period. The caller must have the resourcemanager.folders.undelete permission on the folder.

response.name: The folder has been restored from pending deletion.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Move a Folder

request.description: Moves a folder under a new resource parent. The folder can be moved to another folder or to the organization root. The caller must have the resourcemanager.folders.move permission on both the source folder and the destination parent.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation.
